key_drivers: The U.S. dollar is firmer intraday and the VIX is rising, which is capping broad U.S. equity beta even while the neutral Fed backdrop remains unchanged.
WTI remains supported by a documented EIA crude draw, persistent oil-supply fragility, and elevated geopolitical risk around Middle East shipping routes.
Policy divergence still favors FX outside Europe: the March RBA hike and the BoJ normalization story remain constructive for AUD and JPY, while the ECB is better characterized as a neutral hold.
Growth data remain soft but not recessionary. GDPNow is 1.2%, SOX is still rising, and copper is modestly higher, leaving the cross-asset picture mixed rather than fully defensive.

stale_sources: FRED DFII10 and HY OAS reflect normal publication lag versus the report timestamp.
World Gold Council ETF-flow detail could not be read in-session beyond the existence of the March 2026 update.
EIA inventory detail is the latest official weekly report for the week ended April 10, 2026 pending the next release on April 22.
fallbacks_used: TradingView was used for DXY, SOX, MOVE, and copper live direction, including copper because the preferred Investing.com page was blocked in this environment.
Accessible secondary commentary was used to classify the ECB as a neutral hold after the targeted ECB page path, Reuters, and another secondary article were unavailable in-session.
The full EIA weekly petroleum report PDF was downloaded directly from EIA after the landing page did not expose the crude inventory delta in extracted text.
overnight_changes: DXY was up 0.36% on the day.
VIX was up 3.34% on the day but remained below 20.
MOVE was up 4.23% on the day.
SOX was up 0.50% on the day.
Copper was up 0.07% on the day.

imminent: EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report on April 22, 2026
Any fresh Strait of Hormuz or Middle East shipping disruption headlines
near_term: Next Atlanta Fed GDPNow update on April 29, 2026
background: BoJ normalization remains supportive for JPY
ECB hold meets softer euro-area growth
